title: Expose coarse geo via server endpoint

testFiles.description: Tests for the /api/geo Vercel serverless endpoint

tests.name: returns country and region when geolocation headers are present
tests.description: When Vercel geolocation() returns both country and region, the endpoint should return them in the JSON response.
acceptance_criteria: Country header is read from Vercel environment
Region header is read from Vercel environment
Geo data is exposed via /api/geo endpoint

tests.name: returns null values when geolocation headers are missing
tests.description: When Vercel geolocation() returns undefined/empty values, the endpoint should return { country: null, region: null }.
acceptance_criteria: App works if geo data is unavailable

tests.name: returns partial data when only country is available
tests.description: When only country is present in geolocation, region should be null.
acceptance_criteria: Country header is read from Vercel environment
App works if geo data is unavailable

tests.name: returns partial data when only region is available
tests.description: When only region is present in geolocation, country should be null.
acceptance_criteria: Region header is read from Vercel environment
App works if geo data is unavailable

tests.name: response has Content-Type application/json
tests.description: The response must include the correct Content-Type header.
acceptance_criteria: Geo data is exposed via /api/geo endpoint

tests.name: response has Cache-Control no-store
tests.description: The response must include cache-control: no-store to prevent caching of geo data.
acceptance_criteria: Endpoint uses cache-control: no-store

tests.name: does not expose city, latitude, longitude, or other fine-grained data
tests.description: The response must contain only country and region keys — no city, lat, lng, or other fine-grained geo data.
acceptance_criteria: Endpoint returns only coarse values (country, region)

tests.name: passes request to geolocation helper
tests.description: The handler must pass the incoming request to geolocation() so Vercel can extract geo headers.
acceptance_criteria: Country header is read from Vercel environment
Region header is read from Vercel environment

testFiles.description: Tests for the client-side geo fetch module

tests.name: returns geo data on successful fetch
tests.description: When /api/geo responds successfully, fetchGeoHint should return the parsed country and region.
acceptance_criteria: Client does not access request headers directly

tests.name: returns null values on network error
tests.description: When fetch throws a network error, fetchGeoHint should return { country: null, region: null }.
acceptance_criteria: App works if geo data is unavailable

tests.name: returns null values on non-200 response
tests.description: When the endpoint returns a non-200 status, fetchGeoHint should return null values.
acceptance_criteria: App works if geo data is unavailable

tests.name: returns null values on malformed JSON
tests.description: When the endpoint returns invalid JSON, fetchGeoHint should return null values.
acceptance_criteria: App works if geo data is unavailable

tests.name: fetches /api/geo endpoint
tests.description: The client must fetch from /api/geo, not access headers directly.
acceptance_criteria: Client does not access request headers directly
Geo data is exposed via /api/geo endpoint

tests.name: does not use localStorage, sessionStorage, or cookies
tests.description: The geo fetch module must not use any forbidden storage APIs.
acceptance_criteria: App works if geo data is unavailable

tests.name: returns null values when response has missing fields
tests.description: When the endpoint returns an object missing country or region, fetchGeoHint should default missing fields to null.
acceptance_criteria: App works if geo data is unavailable
